report_name: Carnival Cruise Line Hit by ShinyHunters; Breach Affects Nearly 6 Million

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A notorious extortion group that claimed responsibility for the data breach at Carnival Corporation. They gained access to IT systems using social engineering to deceive an employee, leading to the theft of personal information of nearly 6 million individuals.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The initial attack vector was social engineering, where an attacker manipulated a Carnival employee into providing access. The report suggests this could have been a phishing email, vishing call, or smishing text.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: As a result of successful social engineering, the attacker gained control of a legitimate employee account to access internal IT systems.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The ShinyHunters group exfiltrated the collected personal information from Carnival's network to their own servers to facilitate their extortion-based model.

attack_patterns.name: Device Registration
attack_patterns.description: A suggested hunting hint is to monitor for MFA re-enrollment, which indicates an attacker may be attempting to register their own device to a compromised account to maintain persistent access.

identities.name: Carnival Corporation
identities.description: The world's largest cruise operator, which suffered a data breach affecting nearly 6 million individuals due to a social engineering attack by ShinyHunters in April 2026.

identities.name: Charter Communications
identities.description: A telecommunications company mentioned as another recent victim of the ShinyHunters group, providing context to the threat actor's activities.

indicators.name: Anomalous Remote Access Login
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Remote Access Login
id: 2b9c1f8a-1e3d-4b7c-8a2d-9f6e5c4b3a1d
status: experimental
description: Monitors for high-risk sign-ins which could indicate a compromised employee account being used from an unusual location or under anomalous conditions.
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/05/carnival-confirms-data-breach-impacting-nearly-6-million
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    RiskLevelDuringSignIn: 'high'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects anomalous remote access logins, such as from unusual geographic locations or at odd hours, which could indicate a compromised employee account as seen in the Carnival breach.

indicators.name: High Volume Data Exfiltration Detected by DLP
indicators.pattern:
title: High Volume Data Exfiltration Detected by DLP
id: 8d3e2f1c-9a5b-4d7e-8c1a-0b9e8d7c6f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects a high volume of sensitive data being exfiltrated, as flagged by a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) system. This could indicate a data theft attempt following an account compromise.
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/05/carnival-confirms-data-breach-impacting-nearly-6-million
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1567
  - attack.exfiltration
level: high
logsource:
  category: dlp
detection:
  selection:
    RuleName|contains:
      - 'Mass PII Export'
      - 'Bulk Customer Record Download'
      - 'Anomalous Data Egress'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects alerts from Data Loss Prevention (DLP) systems for large, unauthorized exfiltration of data, which is a key step in data theft breaches like the one executed by ShinyHunters.

indicators.name: Suspicious MFA Device Re-enrollment
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious MFA Device Re-enrollment
id: 5a1b4c3d-2e9f-4a8b-7c6d-1e0f9b8a7d6c
status: experimental
description: Detects an attempt by a user to re-enroll their Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) device. This can be a strong indicator of an account takeover attempt where the attacker is trying to register their own device.
references:
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/05/carnival-confirms-data-breach-impacting-nearly-6-million
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-29
tags:
  - attack.t1098.005
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: auditlogs
detection:
  selection:
    OperationName: 'User registered security info'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects MFA device re-enrollment activity, which can be an indicator of an account takeover attempt where an attacker registers their own device to a compromised account.

relationships.source_name: High Volume Data Exfiltration Detected by DLP
